explanation: The requirement states that when the ventilator is in Standby Mode, it must be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for both ventilation and alarms before the patient is connected and ventilation begins. Using the given atomic propositions, 'StandbyMode' captures the system being in Standby Mode, 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' indicates ventilation parameters are adjustable, and 'alarmSettingsChanged' indicates alarm settings could be adjusted. 'patientConnected' captures that the patient is not yet plugged into the system, and 'ventilating' represents active ventilation (which must not yet have started). The chosen structure expresses that while in Standby Mode and before the patient is connected and ventilation is started, the relevant parameters must be adjustable/adjusted. Note that the boolean expression ensures this only applies before both patient connection and ventilation have occurred, matching the intent that adjustments must be possible 'before connecting to the patient and starting ventilation.'
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation.
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation.
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The requirement states that in Standby Mode and before connecting to the patient or starting ventilation, it must be possible to adjust all user-controlled ventilation and alarm parameters. The atomic propositions indicate 'StandbyMode' for Standby, 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' for ventilation parameters adjustability, 'alarmSettingsChanged' for alarm parameter adjustability, 'patientConnected' for connection to the patient, and 'ventilating' for ventilation started. Using these, the structured NL asserts that while in Standby Mode and before the system is connected to the patient or ventilation is started, it must always be possible to adjust the ventilation and alarm parameters. There are no references to durations or nested triggers; the requirement is a state invariant for a specific system mode and before transitions to treatment.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The requirement states that when the system is in Standby Mode (which means it is not yet ventilating the patient), all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms must be adjustable, but only in this 'pre-connection to patient' state. The atomic propositions given include 'StandbyMode' (the mode context), 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' (ventilator parameters adjustable), and 'alarmSettingsChanged' (change of alarm settings). The property should capture that, while in Standby Mode and before connecting the patient (so before 'patientConnected'), it must be possible to make adjustments to these parameters; after connection, one is no longer in the strictly 'before connection' context. This matches the natural language intent.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation.
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: only while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The input requirement states that when the system is in Standby Mode (ventilation off and before the patient is connected), it must be possible for the user to adjust all user-controlled ventilation and alarm parameters. The atomic propositions 'StandbyMode' (for system state), 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' (for ability to adjust ventilation parameters), and 'alarmSettingsChanged' (for ability to adjust alarm settings) best capture the intended meaning. The phrase 'before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation' suggests these adjustments are allowed exclusively before those actions (i.e., only before 'patientConnected' or 'ventilating'). Thus, the structured NL property is set so that only while the system is in Standby Mode and before the patient is connected or ventilation started, it is always possible to adjust the ventilation and alarm parameters ('ventilationParamsAdjustable | alarmSettingsChanged'). This representation captures both the timing and the enablement intent of the original requirement.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: only while StandbyMode & !(patientConnected | ventilating), always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable | alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The requirement states that while the ventilator is in Standby Mode (ventilation is off and preparing for patient connection), it should be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for both ventilation and alarms, but only before the patient is connected and ventilation is started. The most relevant atomic propositions for capturing this are: StandbyMode (system is in standby), ventilationParamsAdjustable (ventilation parameters are adjustable), alarmSettingsChanged (any change in alarm settings), patientConnected (patient is connected), and ventilating (ventilation is occurring). To capture the intent, we specify that, while StandbyMode and neither patientConnected nor ventilating are true, both ventilation parameters and alarm settings must be adjustable (i.e., it is possible to adjust them). Thus, we create an expression that ensures parameter adjustment is possible only under these circumstances, and use a structured natural language template that is best suited for this: 'while bool_exp1, always satisfy bool_exp3'.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & (alarmSettingsChanged | enterAlarmThresholds)

explanation: The input requirement specifies that when in Standby Mode (ventilator not yet connected to the patient and not ventilating), all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms must be adjustable. The corresponding atomic propositions to express this are: 'StandbyMode' (system is in Standby Mode) and 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' (ventilation parameters are adjustable) and 'alarmSettingsChanged' (any change of alarm settings). To capture the possibility of adjustment (i.e., "shall be possible to adjust"), we use the property template: 'while bool_exp1, always satisfy bool_exp3', meaning that all adjustment actions are always possible while the system is in Standby Mode. Since there is no atomic proposition indicating a specific action like 'adjustment', but instead a state indicating if parameters are adjustable, we use 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' & 'alarmSettingsChanged' to capture this intent (i.e., it is always possible to change parameters and alarms). The condition 'before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation' aligns with being in StandbyMode (before 'patientConnected' or 'ventilating'), tightly mapping to the requirement substring. Decision2 is inapplicable as there is no separate triggering condition for an action; the requirement is state-based.
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation.
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The requirement states that when the ventilator is in Standby Mode (i.e., before the patient is connected and before starting ventilation), it must be possible to adjust all user-controlled ventilation and alarm parameters. Among the provided atomic propositions, 'StandbyMode' represents the system being in Standby Mode, 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' indicates that ventilation parameters are adjustable, and 'alarmSettingsChanged' or 'enterAlarmThresholds' could relate to adjustment of alarms. However, to be faithful to the atomic propositions, we select 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' as denoting the property that the relevant parameters must be adjustable. Since the user's adjustments should be possible during Standby Mode and prior to connecting to the patient, the structured NL property should express that while StandbyMode is true and patient is not connected ('!patientConnected'), ventilation parameters are adjustable. This is accurately captured by 'while StandbyMode & !patientConnected, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable'.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation.

explanation: The requirement specifies that, when the system is in Standby Mode (ventilation off, preparing to ventilate and patient not yet connected), it must be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms. The atomic propositions available for these actions are 'ventilationParamsAdjustable' and 'alarmSettingsChanged'. To capture the intent faithfully, we focus on the ability to adjust these parameters while in StandbyMode, before the patient is connected ('patientConnected') and before ventilation actually starts ('ventilating'). The selected structured NL templates thus use 'while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating', and the main requirement is that ventilation and alarm parameters are always adjustable in this situation (i.e., 'ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged'). No explicit temporal duration is mentioned, so the property is set to 'always satisfy' for this period.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The input requirement states that when the ventilator is in Standby Mode (not yet ventilating), the user must be able to adjust all ventilation and alarm parameters before the patient is connected and ventilation starts. We model this as: whenever StandbyMode is true and patientConnected and ventilating are both false, then both ventilationParamsAdjustable and alarmSettingsChanged must always be possible (this is best captured by eventually satisfy (ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged), since at each stage in Standby mode before connecting the patient and starting ventilation, adjustments may occur at different times, but must always be possible). This structured NL property captures the intent that parameter adjustment is guaranteed and allowed only when the system is in Standby and before connection or ventilation.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged

explanation: The requirement says that in Standby Mode (Ventilation Off), the user must be able to adjust all ventilation and alarm parameters before the patient is connected and before ventilation is started. The structured NL property captures this by stating: (1) while StandbyMode and not patientConnected and not ventilating, (2) it must always be possible to adjust ventilation parameters, and (3) it must always be possible to adjust alarm settings. This fully reflects the intent that, under the given conditions, parameter adjustment is possible.
decision1_substring: In Standby Mode ... before connecting to the patient and starting patient ventilation
decision3_substring: it shall be possible to adjust all user-controlled parameters for ventilation and alarms
output_structured_natural_language: while StandbyMode & !patientConnected & !ventilating, always satisfy ventilationParamsAdjustable & alarmSettingsChanged
